items.headline: Fed holds at 3.50-3.75% under new Chair Warsh but dot plot flips hawkish (median 2026 to ~3.8%, possible hike); S&P ends week ~7,500 (+1.6%, 9th straight gain), QQQ near record ~$740, 10Y ~4.46%; US-Iran peace deal signed
Y Combinator S26 Demo Day wraps June 16 — ~196 startups (AI agents/defense/robotics/infra), new $500K-in-USDC standard deal; standouts Uno Wallet, Silmaril
Odyssey raises $310M Series B at $1.45B for AI world models — led by Natural Capital with Amazon, AMD Ventures, GV, EQT, IQT; week's biggest round
US government orders Anthropic to suspend Fable 5 and Mythos 5 worldwide via export-control directive; Opus 4.8 stays live; Anthropic opens Seoul office days later
Sarvam becomes India's newest AI unicorn — $234M Series B first close at $1.5B, led by HCLTech ($150M, >10% stake) with Bessemer, Khosla, Peak XV
DeepSeek closes first outside round (~$7.4B) at $50B+ via founder-controlled LP with no voting rights, 5-year lockup; Tencent, CATL, national AI fund back
Twenty raises $100M Series B at $1B led by Accel — 'America's first VC-backed cyber-warfare startup' (offensive cyber for US military/IC)
Atom Computing raises $100M Series C (>$300M total) led by Third Point Ventures + signed LOI with US Dept. of Commerce for $100M — fault-tolerant neutral-atom quantum
Salesforce Summer '26: Agentforce Multi-Agent Orchestration goes GA on ~$800M ARR base; Cognizant wires ServiceNow agents into Neuro control plane
Hydra Host raises $100M for GPU marketplace with NVIDIA, ARK, Comcast Ventures, Founders Fund participating — AI-compute infrastructure financing
OpenAI launches Partner Network — $150M to certify ~300,000 consultants (Select/Advanced/Elite tiers) after restructuring Microsoft deal to sell direct
Pogačar opens 2026 Tour de Suisse with ~70km solo Stage 1 win (by 2:14 over Carapaz), seizes GC — 10th win in 12 race days ahead of Tour de France (July 4 start)
Nate Herk (w/ Cole): 'How to Build Effective Claude Code Agents in 2026' — planning + verification system for directing coding agents
Cambridge Judge Business School: 'Why human expertise still matters in the age of AI certainty' — the 'artificial certainty' expertise-illusion framing
Baseten raises $1.5B Series F at $13B valuation (two tranches $13B/$11B) led by Altimeter, Conviction, Spark — 4th raise in 18 months, ~20x YoY revenue, >1B inference requests/day
OpenAI previews GPT-5.6 family (Sol/Terra/Luna) to ~20 government-approved companies; Sol sets SOTA on Terminal-Bench 2.1, strongest safety stack to date
Anthropic accuses Alibaba (Qwen lab) of illicitly accessing Claude via ~25,000 fake accounts / 28.8M queries (Apr–Jun) for 'adversarial distillation'; briefs White House & senators
Trump administration (Commerce/Lutnick) authorizes limited re-release of Anthropic's Mythos 5 to ~100 trusted US firms and federal agencies; Fable 5 stays barred
Markets: Nasdaq -4.5% on the week (QQQ ~$707, off ~$745 record), S&P -1.9% to ~7,354, 10Y eased to ~4.38% on tech rotation + OpenAI IPO-delay report + Apple/Microsoft price hikes; Micron +16%
CRED raises $900M Series H at $4.5B led by Meta (minority stake); founder Kunal Shah steps back from CEO to lead WhatsApp internationally, Miten Sampat interim CEO

General Intuition raises $320M Series A at $2.3B led by Khosla Ventures (General Catalyst, Hedosophia, Bezos Expeditions, Nico Rosberg) — world models trained on Medal gameplay clips; already raising Series B
Stark Defence raises €500M at €3.5B+ co-led by Sequoia and Founders Fund — Berlin drone/defense startup up from ~$500M (Aug 2025) in under a year
a16z leads seed for Mirendil (autonomous AI-for-R&D models that loop over research/engineering problems); founding team from Google, Anthropic, xAI

H1 2026 VC structure: median seed pre-money +19% to ~$16M while seed volume -29% YoY (flight to quality); ~6 'Giga VCs' (a16z, Thrive, Lightspeed, Sequoia, Founders Fund, General Catalyst) dominate financing
items.source: Inc. / HSBC Innovation Banking / SVB State of the Markets

items.headline: Enterprise agents productizing: NVIDIA opens AI-agent toolkit with 16+ software partners (Adobe, Atlassian, SAP, Salesforce, ServiceNow, Siemens); agent spend forecast ~$206B 2026 (+139% YoY); Fivetran index — only ~15% of orgs production-ready
MIT 'AI and Society Forum' (June 23): David Autor reframes AI's labor threat as expertise scarcity, not job loss — automating expert tasks devalues expertise, automating routine work can elevate it
Claude Code late-June release notes — /rewind (resume before /clear), finer auto-mode controls, ~37% lower streaming CPU, MCP/OAuth reliability fixes

Pogačar seals Tour de Suisse GC (June 17-21) by ~7 min over Carapaz with 3 stage wins; pre-Tour bike reveals begin — Colnago sub-550g TT2, leaked Specialized Tarmac SL9, new UCI 400mm min bar-width rule
items.source: Velo / BikeRadar / Cycling Up To Date
